channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lahore woman remanded for five days in case of alleged murder of her three children
item.description: A Lahore court on Friday sent a woman, accused of killing her three young children, into police custody on a five-day physical remand. The incident took place near Shah Jamal Colony, where the childre...

item.title: Afghan asylum seeker detained after alleged harassment near Surrey primary school raises local safety concerns
item.description: An Afghan asylum seeker in his early twenties has been detained under the Mental Health Act following allegations of harassment near a primary school in Laleham, Surrey. Residents reported that the ma...

item.title: Exploring the Futuristic Design of No Vacancy Bar in New Delhi
item.description: No Vacancy, a bar designed by Studiio Dangg in New Delhi, transforms a narrow space into an immersive, experimental environment. The design leans into the room's slender proportions, with curved walls...

item.title: Lawyer says VP Sara Duterte’s cash assets were grouped under ‘others’ in SALN filings
item.description: Nilinaw ng abogado ni Vice President Sara Duterte na ang umano’y kawalan ng nakasaad na cash on hand at bank deposits sa kanyang Statements of Assets, Liabilities, and Net Worth (SALN) mula 2019 hangg...

item.title: UK Implements Historic Smoking Ban for Future Generations
item.description: The UK government has passed a landmark law that will gradually prohibit the sale of cigarettes to anyone born after 2008. Starting in January 2026, this rolling age ban will ensure that children and ...

item.title: AFL CEO criticises Appeals Board over reduced suspension for Lance Collard
item.description: AFL CEO Andrew Dillon has expressed strong disapproval over the Appeals Board’s decision to significantly reduce Lance Collard’s suspension following a homophobic slur. The suspension was initially se...

item.title: UN warns of likely strong El Nino developing by mid-2026 with global temperature impacts
item.description: The United Nations’ weather agency has indicated that El Nino conditions are expected to return by the middle of 2026, with early signs already visible in the Pacific Ocean. According to the World Met...

item.title: UK Oil Refinery Owner Linked to Russian Loans Moves Funds Offshore Amid Sanctions Concerns
item.description: Essar Energy, the Indian-owned conglomerate behind the Stanlow refinery in Cheshire, has come under scrutiny for transferring billions of dollars in Russian loans to an offshore subsidiary in Mauritiu...

item.title: Kenya to Support Uganda’s Oil Refinery Project with Strategic Investment
item.description: Kenya's President William Ruto recently announced that Kenya plans to invest in Uganda's oil refinery, a move aimed at enhancing regional energy collaboration and supporting long-term infrastructure p...

item.title: Practical steps needed to improve healthcare delivery in South Africa
item.description: For many South Africans, accessing healthcare is not a straightforward process; it involves navigating long waiting times, overcrowded facilities, and inefficient referral pathways. Around 84% of the ...

item.title: Trump Orders US Navy to Destroy Iranian Boats Laying Mines in Hormuz
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the United States Navy to destroy any Iranian vessels caught laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz. This announcement came after tensions increased following the US N...

item.title: Thailand Supreme Court Orders Trial of 44 Opposition Lawmakers Over Monarchy Law Reform Attempt
item.description: Thailand's Supreme Court has ruled that 44 current and former opposition lawmakers will face trial for their 2021 attempt to amend the country's strict lese-majeste law, which prohibits criticism of t...

item.title: CAAT Pension Plan Posts 8.4% Return Amid Governance Review
item.description: The CAAT Pension Plan reported an 8.4% return for 2025, driven primarily by gains in publicly traded stocks, although this fell short of its internal benchmark of 11.2%, mainly due to underperformance...

item.title: How Canada Supports Affordable Investment Advice for DIY Investors
item.description: A recent report from the C.D. Howe Institute highlights Canada's success in providing affordable and limited investment advice to individuals with less than $100,000 to invest. The regulatory environm...

item.title: India rebukes Trump for sharing derogatory remarks amid birthright citizenship debate
item.description: The Government of India has strongly criticised remarks describing India as a ‘hellhole’, which were shared by United States President Donald Trump on social media. The Ministry of External Affairs (M...

item.title: Nigel Farage outlines Reform UK’s election strategy and views on welfare, energy and political divides
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, has set out an assertive political agenda ahead of upcoming local elections, arguing that Britain’s primary social divide is no longer based on class, race or wealth...

item.title: Redwood Materials COO departs as company restructures and cuts workforce
item.description: Redwood Materials, the battery recycling and energy storage company founded by former Tesla CTO JB Straubel, is undergoing a period of significant organizational change marked by executive departures ...

item.title: Tokyo government allows workers to wear shorts amid rising temperatures
item.description: Pinayagan ng pamahalaan ng Tokyo ang ilang government workers na magsuot ng shorts sa halip na tradisyonal na business suits bilang bahagi ng kanilang hakbang para harapin ang tumitinding init ng pana...

item.title: Research suggests ancient giant octopus lived in prehistoric oceans
item.description: A recent study has shed light on the existence of a giant octopus species that once inhabited Earth's oceans. Researchers analyzed fossil evidence and other paleontological data, revealing that these ...

item.title: King Charles III's U.S. Visit Highlights Transatlantic Tensions and Diplomatic Challenges
item.description: King Charles III is set to make his first state visit to the United States as monarch, accompanied by Queen Camilla. The four-day trip aims to celebrate the 250th anniversary of American independence ...
